docs.title: TAQ Cleaner
docs.text:
Clean and classify crypto exchange TAQ (Trade and Quote) data.

pip install taq-cleaner
taq clean --input trades.parquet --output clean.parquet

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Ingest — Read trade/quote data from Binance, Coinbase, Kraken (CSV or Parquet)
  • Clean — Timestamp alignment, flickering quote filtering, trade-through removal, out-of-sequence sorting
  • Classify — Bulk Volume Classification (BVC) for trade direction and toxicity
  • Export — Clean, enriched Parquet/CSV with classification columns
  • CLI — taq clean, taq info, taq schema, taq benchmark
  • Web API — Upload and process via HTTP

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Health check endpoint.

Response: {"status": "ok"}

docs.title: POST /clean
docs.text:
Upload a TAQ file and receive cleaned + classified data.

Request: - file (multipart form) — .csv or .parquet file - bucket_size (form, optional) — Volume bucket size (default: 1000) - skip_clean (form, optional) — Skip cleaning stage - skip_classify (form, optional) — Skip classification stage - to_csv (form, optional) — Return CSV instead of Parquet

Response: Binary file download with the cleaned data.

Headers: - Content-Disposition — Filename for the download - X-TAQ-Rows — Number of rows processed

Status codes: - 200 — Success - 400 — Invalid file format or missing file - 413 — File too large (max 500 MB) - 422 — Validation error

docs.title: Starting the server
docs.text: uvicorn taq_cleaner.web.app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

docs.title: Benchmarks
docs.text: Performance benchmarks measure the throughput of each pipeline stage.

docs.title: Running
docs.text: taq benchmark --rows 1_000_000

docs.title: Target
docs.text: The V1 target is 100M rows processed in under 60 seconds on a standard dev machine (M1 MacBook Pro or equivalent).

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text: Stage Description Ingest Read and parse raw data, detect exchange, normalize schema Clean Flickering filter, trade-through removal, timestamp alignment Classify BVC computation (bucketing, VPIN, confidence) Total Full pipeline end-to-end

docs.title: Classification (BVC)
docs.text: Bulk Volume Classification (BVC) estimates trade direction without requiring precise trade-to-quote timestamp matching.

docs.title: Why BVC over Lee-Ready?
docs.text:
Lee-Ready classifies each trade individually by comparing its price to the prevailing quote midpoint. In modern HFT environments, this fails because:

  • Quotes update faster than trades can be timestamped
  • Flickering quotes create spurious quote midpoints
  • Reporting latency misaligns trades with their "true" quote

BVC avoids these problems by classifying volume in aggregate over volume-synchronized buckets.

docs.title: How BVC Works
docs.text:
1. Partition trades into volume buckets (e.g., 1,000 contracts each)
  2. Compute the standardized price return within each bucket
  3. Use the cumulative normal distribution to estimate buy/sell proportion:

$$ \text{Buy Volume} = \text{Total Volume} \times \Phi(Z) $$

where $Z$ is the standardized return and $\Phi$ is the standard normal CDF.

  1. Compute the Order Imbalance: $$ \text{Imbalance} = \frac{|\text{Buy Volume} - \text{Sell Volume}|}{\text{Total Volume}} $$

  2. Compute VPIN (Volume-Synchronized Probability of Informed Trading) as a moving average of imbalance over the last N buckets.

  3. Confidence = $1 - \text{Imbalance}$

docs.title: Output Columns
docs.text: Column Description bucket_index Sequential bucket number buy_volume Estimated buy-initiated volume sell_volume Estimated sell-initiated volume total_volume Total volume in the bucket imbalance Order flow imbalance (0-1) vpin VPIN metric (moving average of imbalance) confidence Classification confidence (1 - imbalance) taq_cleaner_version Version used for processing classification_method Always "BVC" in V1

docs.title: Cleaning Pipeline
docs.text: The cleaning pipeline applies four transformations to raw TAQ data:

docs.title: 1. Timestamp Alignment
docs.text:
Trades are aligned to their prevailing quote using an as-of join. For each trade, the most recent quote within a configurable tolerance window is attached. This is critical for accurate trade classification.

from taq_cleaner.core.clean import align_timestamps

aligned = align_timestamps(trades, quotes, tolerance_ns=1_000_000)

docs.title: 2. Flickering Quote Filter
docs.text:
Quotes that update faster than a minimum lifespan are removed. These "flickering quotes" are artifacts of HFT quote stuffing and degrade Lee-Ready classification accuracy.

from taq_cleaner.core.clean import filter_flickering_quotes

clean_quotes = filter_flickering_quotes(quotes, min_lifespan_ns=1_000_000)

docs.title: 3. Trade-Through Removal
docs.text:
Trades executed outside the prevailing bid-ask spread are removed. These typically represent data errors or trades from alternative execution venues.

from taq_cleaner.core.clean import filter_trade_throughs

clean_trades = filter_trade_throughs(trades)

docs.title: 4. Out-of-Sequence Sorting
docs.text:
Events arriving out of chronological order are re-sorted by timestamp. This happens with data from fragmented exchange architectures.

from taq_cleaner.core.clean import sort_out_of_sequence

sorted_df = sort_out_of_sequence(df)

docs.title: taq clean
docs.text:
Run the full cleaning and classification pipeline.

taq clean INPUT OUTPUT [options]

Arguments: - INPUT — Path to input .csv or .parquet file - OUTPUT — Path for the cleaned output file

Options: - --bucket-size, -b — Volume bucket size for BVC (default: 1000) - --skip-clean — Skip the cleaning stage - --skip-classify — Skip the classification stage - --csv — Output as CSV instead of Parquet

docs.title: taq info
docs.text:
Display summary information about a TAQ file.

taq info INPUT

Output: Row count, detected exchange, time range, column list.

docs.title: taq schema
docs.text:
Detect and display the schema of a TAQ file.

taq schema INPUT

Output: Detected exchange (if recognized), column names with data types.

docs.title: taq benchmark
docs.text:
Run a performance benchmark.

taq benchmark [--rows N]

Options: - --rows, -n — Number of rows for the benchmark dataset (default: 1,000,000)

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text: pip install taq-cleaner

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/taq-cleaner/taq-cleaner
cd taq-cleaner
pip install -e .

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.12+
  • Polars, Typer, FastAPI, Pydantic (installed automatically)

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text: pip install taq-cleaner

docs.title: 2. Clean a Parquet file
docs.text: taq clean --input data/trades.parquet --output data/clean.parquet

docs.title: 3. Inspect the results
docs.text:
taq info --input data/clean.parquet
taq schema --input data/trades.parquet

docs.title: 4. Try the web API
docs.text:
uvicorn taq_cleaner.web.app:app
# then POST a file to http://localhost:8000/clean
